authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

reaches.name: Little Truckee River — Stampede Dam to Boca Reservoir

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(80) — Little Truckee River
sources.quotedText: (80) Little Truckee River (Sierra and Nevada cos.) from Stampede Reservoir Dam downstream to Boca Reservoir. All year. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout.

sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85 — Trout (General Statewide Regulations, Streams/Rivers)
sources.quotedText: (2) All inland streams, rivers, and canals, except those listed in Sections 7.40(b) and 7.50(b), are open to fishing from the last Saturday in April through November 15 with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit, with no gear restrictions. From November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April, a zero-trout bag limit applies, and only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used for all species of fish. In waters where the bag limit for trout is zero, trout must be released unharmed and should not be removed from the water.

sources.title: 2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (Updated 06/01/2026)
sources.quotedText: (80) Little Truckee River (Sierra and Nevada cos.) from Stampede Reservoir Dam downstream to Boca Reservoir. All year. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

endSpec.verbatim: Friday preceding the last Saturday in April

regulations.humanSummary: Artificial lures with barbless hooks; no bait

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout/day, 10 in possession during the take season (last Sat Apr–Nov 15) — Sierra District statewide stream default, elsewhere on the river outside the Stampede-Boca reach

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.
